capabilities.title: Toggle a GPIO output (LED blink)
capabilities.pattern: Enable the port clock (__HAL_RCC_GPIO<port>_CLK_ENABLE), configure the pin as GPIO_MODE_OUTPUT_PP, then HAL_GPIO_TogglePin(GPIO<port>, GPIO_PIN_<n>) on a HAL_Delay() cadence. Portable raw HAL — no vendor BSP.
capabilities.verify: Scope/LA on the pin, or watch the onboard LED — one edge per HAL_Delay period.

capabilities.title: Configure the system clock (SystemClock_Config)
capabilities.pattern: HAL_RCC_OscConfig() to bring up the oscillator (MSI by default on U5), then HAL_RCC_ClockConfig() to route SYSCLK and set the AHB/APB prescalers with the correct FLASH latency. The family template ships a working default; retargeting to a specific frequency is a refman-verified sequence (voltage scaling + flash latency must track the target HCLK).
capabilities.verify: Read SystemCoreClock after SystemCoreClockUpdate(), or measure MCO.

gotchas.note: Main SRAM is 768K @ 0x20000000 with a SEPARATE SRAM4 16K @ 0x28000000 — do not collapse to one 784K region. `_estack` sits at the end of the main RAM.
Hard-float compiler flags (-mfloat-abi=hard) alongside the stock `.fpu softvfp` in the CMSIS startup .s is the normal, correct combo — it links fine, don't 'fix' it.
The modern GCC11+ linker script uses the `(READONLY)` attribute on .ARM.extab/.ARM etc. On GCC<=10 those lines must drop `(READONLY)`; the harness assumes GCC11+.

U5 can boot a TrustZone dual secure/non-secure image, but a plain scaffold is a single non-secure image — it emits no partition_*.h and needs none (system_stm32u5xx.c only references it under __ARM_FEATURE_CMSE).
